description_long: This is a portable Sony Discman CD player with a matte black plastic body and a compact, rectangular form. The top surface features a clear 'Discman' logo and a set of tactile control buttons, including play, stop, and skip, as well as a small display window. Attached to the player is a pair of classic over-ear headphones with orange foam ear pads and a thin black headband, connected via a coiled black cable. The front edge of the player houses the main control interface, including a sliding open/close latch for the disc tray and a visible headphone jack. The overall design is sleek and functional, with subtle metallic accents and a robust, utilitarian build. This object is typical of late 20th-century portable audio devices, designed for personal music listening in urban environments.
description: A black Sony Discman portable CD player with orange foam headphones and visible control buttons, designed for personal audio use. The device features a compact, rectangular shape with a clear logo and a coiled headphone cable.
description_view_0: Front view showing the control buttons, display window, headphone jack, and the attached orange headphones resting on top.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the thickness of the player, the headphone cable connection, and the side profile of the headphones.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the Discman, the headband of the headphones, and the cable trailing off to the side.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the disc tray area, the open/close latch, and the foam ear pads of the headphones.
